title: Save Electricity at Home
summary: Take simple daily actions to reduce electricity use at home.
learnerPromise: You will be able to lower your home electricity use through small habits and choices that are easy to keep up.
whyItMatters: Using less electricity lowers your utility bill and reduces strain on the power grid. Small habits add up to noticeable savings over time.
realLifeUses: Turn off lights and devices when leaving a room
Choose the right temperature setting on your thermostat to avoid overworking the AC or heater
Unplug chargers and appliances that draw power even when switched off
Replace frequently used bulbs with lower-wattage or LED options
result: A short checklist of electricity-saving habits you can use daily.
youWillLearn: Identify which devices and lights use the most electricity in your home
Use natural light and timed usage to cut down on unnecessary power draw
Adjust thermostat settings by a few degrees to reduce heating and cooling costs
Build a short personal checklist of habits to check before bed or when leaving
miniChallenge: Walk through one room in your home and list three things that are using electricity right now that could be turned off or adjusted.
steps: Walk through each room and note what is plugged in, turned on, or running
Turn off lights, fans, and devices in rooms you are not using
Unplug chargers, small appliances, and electronics that are not in use
Check your thermostat and adjust it by 2-3 degrees toward the outdoor temperature
Write down three habits you will try to keep for the next week
completionCriteria: You complete a walkthrough of at least one room and turn off or unplug unused items
You have a written list of three habits you plan to keep
commonProblems: Some devices need to stay on (like refrigerators or medical equipment)
It is easy to forget to turn things off when you are in a hurry
Shared spaces make it hard to control what others leave on
tips: Focus on the biggest users first: heating, cooling, and hot water usually matter most
Use a power strip to turn off several devices at once
If you share a home, post a small reminder note near light switches or the door
Small changes for a full month are more useful than perfect changes for one day
